This Type Certificate Data Sheet (TCDS), which is part of Type Certificate No. A-142, prescribes the 
conditions and limitations under which the product(s) for which the type certificate was granted 
meet(s) the standards of airworthiness required by the Canadian Aviation Regulations.

Bombardier Inc. DHC-8-101, -102, -103, -106 
123 Garratt Boulevard DHC-8-201, -202 
Downsview, Ontario DHC-8-301, -311, -314, -315 
M3K1Y5 DHC-8-400, -401, -402

Items of Equivalent Safety 
1.	 Pilot compartment view FAR 25.773(b)(2).

MODELS -311, -314 and -315 with Change Request CR803S000001 or 
CR803S000002 incorporated). 
Exemptions 
1.	 FAR 25.571(e)(2) Propeller Debris (ref. FAA Exemption No. NM-102; 
TC letter 5010-10-366 (ABP/ A) dated February 10, 1984). 
2.	 FAR 25.807(c)(1) 40 Passenger Configuration - Series 100 and 200; (ref. 
TC letter 5010-10-366 dated March 14, 1986).

25.785(h), Flight Attendants Seats.

February 15, 2007, Emergency Exit Signs, AWM 525.811(g) and 
525.812(b)(1)(i) and (ii) - (See NOTE 6).

established:

MODEL DHC-8-400, -401, -402 (Cont'd) 
Basis of 6. Items of Equivalent Safety
 
Certification
 
(Refer to Transport Canada Issue Paper G-3, dated June 16, 1999) (Cont'd) 
1.	 Reduced Minimum Operating Speed Factors (Various AWM 525 
Requirements). 
2.	 Hydraulic System Testing AWM 525.1435(b)(1) 
3.	 Damage Tolerance Evaluation of Bird Impact AWM 525.571(e)(l) 
7.	 Later Amendments to the Standards 
(Refer to Transport Canada Issue Paper G-5 dated May 13, 1999). 
The DHC-8 Series 400 was certificated as a derivative of the Series 100 
aircraft. The applicable basis of certification includes later FAR Part 25 
Amendments 25-84, 25-85 25-86 and 25-92 which came into effect after the 
date of application for type certification. 
8.	 Optional Requirements 
Compliance with the following additional optional requirements has been 
established: 
Ice Protection:	 AWM 525.1419 
Ditching:	 Compliance with AWM 525.801 has been established 
when the safety equipment requirements of AWM 
525.1411 and the ditching equipment requirements of 
AWM 525.1415 are satisfied. 
9.	 Exemptions 
Exemption No. 013-2007-NCR/RCN dated February 15, 2007, Emergency 
Exit Signs, AWM 525.811 (g) and 525.812(b)(1)(i) and (ii) (See NOTE 6). 
Exemptions No. 2002-04 and NCR/RCN #129-2009 from AWM 525.562 
and 523.785(b) for stretcher installations (See NOTE 7).

Life Limited Parts	 Components which are life limited are listed in the Airworthiness Limitations 
Section of the Maintenance Program listed in Approved Publications 
(See NOTE 2). 
Placards	 All placards listed in de Havilland document 81151001 for the DHC-8-100, 200, 
and 300 series or in documents 81150004 and 81150005 for the DHC-8-400 
series, must be installed in their appropriate location. 
NOTE 1	 The aircraft must be operated in accordance with the appropriate Flight Manual 
listed in Approved Publications. 
NOTE 2	 Compliance with the tasks and intervals specified in the Airworthiness 
Limitations section of the Maintenance Program listed in Approved 
Publications is required to ensure continuing compliance with the Basis of 
Certification. 
NOTE 3	 Modifications required to convert a model DHC-8-101 to a -102, a -102 to a -103, 
a -102/ -103 to a -106, a -311 to a -314, and a -311 to a -315 are identified in the 
Definition of Certified Airplanes AEROC 8.1.AC.llisted in Approved 
Publications.

DATA PERTINENT TO ALL MODELS EXCEPT AS INDICATED (Cont'd) 
NOTE 4	 The DHC-8 Series 200 was certificated as a derivative of the Series 100 aircraft. 
The applicable basis of certification is the same as the Series 100, but the 
manufacturer elected to demonstrate compliance with FAR Part 25, up to 
Amendment 25-66, less the exceptions shown under Basis of Certification, 
Series 200. 
NOTE 5	 Models DHC-8-101, -102, -103, -106, -301, -311 and -314 are in compliance with 
the requirements of the ICAO Annex 16, Second Edition, Volume II, 
Amendment 2, applicable on November 11, 1993 (equivalent to FAR Part 34 
original issue, effective September 10, 1990). 
NOTE 6	 Exemption No. 013-2007-NCRjRCN is issued post type certification to permit 
installation of emergency exit signs that do not comply with the Airworthiness 
Manual (AWM 525) requirements on any of the DHC-8-100j200j300j 400 
aeroplanes intended for export. 
NOTE 7	 Exemption 2002-04 applies to the installation of a stretcher to be used for 
non-ambulatory persons only and is limited to a stretcher manufactured by 
Lifeport Inc. and consisting of portable stretcher assembly Aerostretcher model 
No. 100-4063-6 and mounting adapter Meddeck model no. 100-4618. 
Exemption NCRjRCN #129-2009 applies to the installation of a portable 
medical stretcher, as approved by Transport Canada, to be used for a 
non-ambulatory person or a person who cannot be seated in an aircraft 
passenger seat due to medical reasons 
NOTE 8	 The DHC-8 Series 400 are in compliance with the requirements of the AWM 
516, Change 516-07 - Chapter 4; FAA CFR 14 part 36, Amendment 36-28 ~ Stage 
4; ICAO Annex 16, Volume 1 - Chapter 4. 
-END-
Illes A. Morin 
Chief, Project Management 
National Aircraft Certification 
for Minister of Transport
